prevalence.trend: stable; diagnosis improving with genetic testing access

vulnerablePopulations: patients with limited access to genetic counseling
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical motor/cognitive/psychiatric syndrome with confirmatory genetic testing; supportive neuroimaging; multidisciplinary evaluation

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Not curable; symptom management depends on multidisciplinary access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Genetic testing and counseling concentrated in specialized centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Symptomatic management (chorea, psychiatric symptoms), rehabilitation, caregiver support, and palliative planning
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to neurogenetics services and long-term multidisciplinary support outside major centers
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Specialized university centers can provide diagnosis and multidisciplinary follow-up
